message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH       = process ALL 346 baskets from the outline (NOT one context window).
  FAITHFULNESS= context NLI entailment + STRICT numbers.

BINDING INVARIANTS (from docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md, non-negotiable):
- The faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
- No tool result renders directly. External content re-enters ONLY through the fold-in seam
  (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792).
- Computed numbers render ONLY through the verified lane (tradeoff_modeler ModelSpec / [#calc:]).
  Exploratory execute_python output is BARRED from rendering.
- WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter/cap. LAW VI: every knob env-tunable.

HARD RULES FOR YOU:
- Work ONLY inside undefined (branch undefined). NEVER touch another wheel's worktree.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never round up, never claim a pass you did not observe.
- If something fails, say it failed and paste the real lines.


YOU ARE THE INDEPENDENT GATE (Fable) for the POLARIS "undefined" wheel. Round 1.
You did NOT build this. A builder never grades its own homework. Be adversarial.

FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: undefined

THE TESTER JUST RAN THE REAL CODE AND REPORTED:
{
  "ran": true,
  "command": "cd /home/polaris/wt/tooluse && python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py -v -p no:randomly   # + mutation test + PYTHONPATH=. python /tmp/drive_real.py (real end-to-end drive of the REAL execute_python)",
  "verdict": "PARTIAL",
  "real_output": "$ python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py -v -p no:randomly\nplatform linux -- Python 3.11.10, pytest-8.4.1, pluggy-1.5.0\ncollected 7 items\n\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_failed_execute_python_lands_unfilled_and_is_never_routed_to_retrieval PASSED [ 14%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_successful_execute_python_records_no_gap PASSED [ 28%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_codegen_tools_membership PASSED [ 42%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_execute_passes_real_client_to_execute_python_and_closes_it PASSED [ 57%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_execute_closes_client_when_the_tool_raises PASSED [ 71%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_execute_passes_none_client_for_a_non_codegen_tool PASSED [ 85%]\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py::test_statistical_summary_failure_still_lands_pending PASSED [100%]\n\n============================== 7 passed in 1.73s ===============================\n\n--- MUTATION TEST (revert the fix: client=code_client -> client=None) ---\ntests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py:157: in test_execute_passes_real_client_to_execute_python_and_closes_it\n    assert seen[\"client\"] is not None, \"execute_python must receive a real client (FIX 1)\"\nE   AssertionError: execute_python must receive a real client (FIX 1)\nE   assert None is not None\n========================= 1 failed, 6 passed in 1.88s ==========================\nRESTORED clean\n\n--- REAL END-TO-END DRIVE (real OutlineAgent._execute -> real registered _wrap_execute_python\n    -> real code_executor -> real sandboxed subprocess; only client.generate() HTTP stubbed) ---\nregistry execute_python -> src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry._wrap_execute_python (requires_llm=True)  [NOT a fake]\ncode model that _execute should build: 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro'\n  [stub] client.generate() CALLED -> model='deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro' max_tokens=4096 temp=0.3\n\n===== RUN 1 =====\nRESULT success=True error=None\nmarkdown[:300]=\"{'n': 3, 'mean': 0.0, 'vals': [0, 0, 0]} [CITE:ev_0][CITE:ev_1][CITE:ev_2]\"\nstatistics={}\nclients constructed=1 closed=[1]\ngap ledger todos=[]\nOK: real executor got past the `if not client` guard at tool_registry.py:556\n===== RUN 2 ===== (identical)\n===== RUN 3 ===== (identical)\n\n--- sandbox guard firing on a hostile script (real, not mocked) ---\n[code_executor] Generated script failed validation: Reference to dangerous builtin 'open' is not allowed (reflection/exec-bypass guard)\n\n--- failure path, real ---\nRESULT success=False error='LLM failed to generate a valid script'\ngap ledger todos=[('(compute)', 'UNFILLED')]",
  "metrics": {
    "pytest_passed": 7,
    "pytest_failed": 0,
    "pytest_seconds": 1.73,
    "mutation_tests_killed": 1,
    "real_drive_runs": 3,
    "real_drive_success_true": 3,
    "tests_in_outline_dir_total": 7,
    "openrouter_calls_made": 0,
    "http_429_seen": 0
  },
  "failures": [
    {
      "symptom": "Computed numbers from execute_python are stamped with [CITE:ev_*] evidence citations — the citation lane, which the mission invariant explicitly bars for computed values ('Computed numbers render ONLY through the verified lane / [#calc:]; exploratory execute_python output is BARRED from rendering'). In my real run the derived value 'mean: 0.0, n: 3' came back cited to ev_0/ev_1/ev_2, whose statements are 'eff 80 pct' / 'eff 81 pct' / 'eff 82 pct'. Neither 0.0 nor 3 appears in any cited span, so the provenance stamp is false. strict_verify check (d) should drop it; if it ever does not, a derived number renders with fabricated source attribution.",
      "evidence": "src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py:630  markdown=_cite_inline(summary, ev_ids),   -> REAL output: markdown=\"{'n': 3, 'mean': 0.0, 'vals': [0, 0, 0]} [CITE:ev_0][CITE:ev_1][CITE:ev_2]\"",
      "suspected_component": "tool_registry._wrap_execute_python (result construction)"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "execute_python silently computes over TEXT-ONLY rows. When notebook.data_points is empty, _wrap_execute_python builds its input from evidence_store keeping ONLY {statement, evidence_id, source_url} — every numeric field is dropped. execute_python is registered requires_data=False, so the decide LLM is told it is available with zero data. In my real run the evidence carried value=80/81/82; the sandbox received rows without that key and the script computed mean=0.0 over [0,0,0] — a garbage number that still returned success=True and got citation-stamped.",
      "evidence": "src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py:570  input_data = data_points[:30] if data_points else [ {statement, evidence_id, source_url} ... ]  + tool_registry.py:770 requires_data=False  -> REAL output: \"{'n': 3, 'mean': 0.0, 'vals': [0, 0, 0]}\"",
      "suspected_component": "tool_registry._wrap_execute_python (input_data fallback) / registry ordering: nothing forces extract_numeric_data to run first"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS action-2 P1 CONFIRMED against real code: the computed value still cannot reach the decide LLM. summary_for_llm() — the only notebook read on the outline path — emits 'tool_name [OK/FAILED] (elapsed) — reasoning[:60]' and does NOT include step.result.markdown or statistics. So the agent sees 'execute_python [OK]' and learns nothing. Un-bricking the tool does not yet let a computed number reach the outline. The wheel is correctly left UNSIGNED.",
      "evidence": "src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:142 def summary_for_llm(...) — loop emits only f'  {step.step_number}. {step.tool_name} [{status}] ({step.elapsed_seconds:.1f}s) — {step.reasoning[:60]}'",
      "suspected_component": "analysis_notebook.summary_for_llm (shared with react_agent — why the driver did not touch it)"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "One non-reproducible run of the real drive returned error='No LLM client available' (the exact pre-fix brick) even though a client was constructed and closed. It did NOT reproduce across 3 subsequent clean runs after clearing __pycache__; most likely stale bytecode from the mutation test I had just run. Reporting it because I observed it and cannot prove it was only bytecode.",
      "evidence": "RESULT success=False error='No LLM client available' / clients constructed=1 closed=[1]  — then, after `find . -name __pycache__ -prune -exec rm -rf {} +`, 3/3 runs gave success=True",
      "suspected_component": "test artifact (stale __pycache__), not product code — but unproven"
    }
  ],
  "blockers": [
    "WHEEL IDENTITY BLOCKED: the orchestrator passed the wheel name, branch, worktree and entrypoint as the literal string 'undefined' (variable interpolation failure). /home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined does not exist and there is no branch named 'undefined'. I did NOT guess a wheel at random: I read OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md and tested the one wheel it explicitly leaves UNSIGNED and names as the next action — tooluse, /home/polaris/wt/tooluse, branch bot/outline-tooluse-box @ efe5af3. If Round 1 meant a different wheel, re-dispatch with the name filled in.",
    "Note for the orchestrator: the brief's worktrees /workspace/{compose,outline_agent,outline_tooluse}_wt are root:root and read-only to uid 1000 (no sudo). The real, writable wheel worktrees are under /home/polaris/wt/. Any task template pointing at /workspace/*_wt will fail to write.",
    "NO 429 observed — because I made ZERO OpenRouter calls. I stubbed only client.generate() (the HTTP boundary) and drove every other layer for real, so this run consumed no rate limit and no spend. The corollary: the LLM codegen call itself is the one link in the execute_python chain I did not exercise live."
  ]
}



DO THIS — and READ THE REAL CODE in undefined, do not reason from the summary alone:
1. LINE-BY-LINE READ of the real output above against the real source. Verify the tester's
   claims. Testers are wrong sometimes; a PASS that asserts nothing is a FAIL.
2. ROOT-CAUSE each failure to a MECHANISM at file:line. "It errors" is not a root cause.
   Severity: P0 = faithfulness breach (unsupported number rendered as verified, injection
   obeyed, silent wrong answer). P1 = wrong/unreachable result, undisclosed. P2 = cosmetic.
3. SOTA ASSESSMENT — the honest one. Does this wheel, AS IT ACTUALLY IS RIGHT NOW, beat
   ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on its axis? What is the concrete remaining gap?
   Do not flatter. If we are behind, say we are behind and by what.
4. FIX PLAN: concrete, file:line, front-loaded (most important first). Every step needs an
   ACCEPTANCE OBSERVABLE — the thing the next test run must SEE to prove the fix took.
5. SIGN_OFF: true ONLY if 0 P0 and 0 P1. NEVER force-approve. If the tester was BLOCKED and
   you cannot see real evidence, that is NOT a sign-off.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
